remediations.title: lint desactivado (echo … exit 0)
remediations.fix: Replaced the 'echo … exit 0' lint stub in package.json with a real ESLint run over the governance layer (.claude/hooks, .aiox/task-discovery.js, tests/hooks) using --quiet; added lint:all for full-repo visibility. Fixed 2 real errors surfaced by re-enabling lint: orphan isStrict export in enforce-no-invention.cjs and a useless assignment in task-discovery.js.
remediations.scopeDecision: Governance-layer scope chosen over full-repo: 'eslint .' surfaces 180 legacy errors across 61 files in L1 (.aiox-core/core), packages/web (Next.js) and squads, which are a dedicated cleanup story, out of scope for the Task 2.2 governance remediation. The Art.V gate now verifies real code instead of always passing green.
commits: 525f7f4 fix: enable eslint for Art.V quality gate [Story 5.2]
remediations.verified: npm run lint exits 0 (real ESLint, no warnings/errors in scope)

remediations.title: build script ausente
remediations.fix: Added scripts.build = 'npm run typecheck && npm run kairos:web:build', wiring the canonical Art.V build gate to typecheck plus the existing buildable Next.js target (kairos:web:build). The zero-dep JS backend has no compile step, so typecheck is its build verification.
commits: 687db4a fix: add npm build script for Art.V quality gate [Story 5.2]
remediations.verified: npm run build resolves (no 'Missing script: build'); script chains typecheck + kairos:web:build

remediations.title: frameworkProtection falso
remediations.fix: Set boundary.frameworkProtection = true in core-config.yaml (L3, mutable) to match the 97 active deny rules in settings.json that already protect L1/L2. Removes the contradictory source-of-truth. Also resolves AMB-003.
commits: e16e1de fix: enable framework boundary protection in core-config [Story 5.2]
remediations.verified: frameworkProtection now reads true in core-config.yaml

remediations.title: Mislabeling de categorias
remediations.fix: Task category labeling corrected: task-discovery.js matches task-id domain signal before body text and reorders CATEGORY_RULES so concrete domains (docs/workflow/squad/devops) win over generic action verbs; task-registry.json regenerated. Result: 213 tasks distributed across 13 real categories with no catch-all qa/db collapse (config:10, db:57, arch:8, devops:35, qa:26, workflow:19, feature:21, docs:8, refactor:7, bug:1, squad:19, collaboration:1, release:1).
commits: 585acdb fix: recalibrate task routing — FP-01 threshold + FP-02 categorization [Story 5.2]
remediations.verified: task-registry.json shows 13-category distribution, no qa/db catch-all collapse
remediations.note: Implemented in prior session (commit 585acdb). Re-implementing would violate Art. IV (No Invention) and IDS REUSE — recorded as completed.

remediations.title: Auto-ativação inerte
remediations.fix: Task auto-activation scoring recalibrated: primary-category match weight raised 50->60 so a confident match (60 + keyword + story-start) clears the 70 AUTO_ACTIVATION_THRESHOLD; threshold exported and autoActivate flags surfaced. Adds tests/hooks/task-discovery-routing.test.js (8 tests passing).
commits: 585acdb fix: recalibrate task routing — FP-01 threshold + FP-02 categorization [Story 5.2]
remediations.verified: AUTO_ACTIVATION_THRESHOLD = 70 with weight 60 enabling confident matches to clear it; 8 routing tests pass
remediations.note: Implemented in prior session (commit 585acdb). Re-implementing would violate Art. IV (No Invention) and IDS REUSE — recorded as completed.

remediations.title: Constitution version drift (Art. VII & Art. IV-A enforced but undefined)
remediations.fix: NOT modified directly. constitution.md is L1 (NEVER modify). Requires @aiox-master *propose-modification with IDS impact assessment to formally add Art. VII (Framework Boundary) and Art. IV-A (Incremental Development) and bump version 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0.
remediations.logMessage: V-DRIFT-004 requires @aiox-master *propose-modification (L1 boundary respected, constitution.md not touched). Not a blocker for Phase 2.2 pass per SYNC-FINDINGS gate verdict.
